This review provided an overview of incorporating
human feedback into LLMs, with a focus on subjec-
tive preferences and values that lack ‘ground truth
We have witnessed two notable shifts
in the field from past to present—first, a move away
second, more use of direct human feedback which
surpasses the limitations of user simulations or au-
While the shift to incorporate human voices di-
troduces new challenges that require careful nav-
Some challenges are more tractable than
others—for example, practitioners will always have
unstable and idiosyncratic preferences across end
users of their model, but can take practical steps to
better approximate this distribution by diversifying
tegrity and reliability of research efforts.
